title: crotalaria pterocaula var. genistella
intro: Crotalaria pterocaula var. genistella, also known as the bush calabar or calabar bean, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to tropical Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a toxic compound called rotenone, that has been used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of the crotalaria pterocaula var. genistella plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying the seeds and using them in teas, infusions, or as a powder for medicinal purposes.
